natural_language: Eight blocks — A through H — have been stacked into one massive pillar. Reading from the floor to the ceiling: A is on the table, B on A, C on B, D on C, E on D, F on E, G on F, and finally H at the very top. So it's an eight-story tower. Only H at the peak is exposed and grabbable. The robot arm starts empty-handed. Your task is to completely reverse this entire tower from top to bottom. The new arrangement should read: H on the table, then G on H, then F on G, then E on F, then D on E, then C on D, then B on C, and finally A at the summit. In other words, flip it — what was the roof becomes the foundation, what was the foundation becomes the roof, and everything in between slides accordingly.
